game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
TIS-100 is an open-ended programming game by Zachtronics, the creators of SpaceChem and Infinifactory, in which you rewrite corrupted code segments to repair the TIS-100 and unlock its secrets. It’s the assembly language programming game you never asked for!

TIS-100 is an open-ended programming game by Zachtronics, the creators of SpaceChem and Infinifactory, in which you rewrite corrupted code segments to repair the TIS-100 and unlock its secrets. It’s the assembly language programming game you never asked for!



The Tessellated Intelligence Systems TIS-100 is a massively parallel computer architecture comprised of non-uniformly interconnected heterogeneous nodes. The TIS-100 is ideal for applications requiring complex data stream processing, such as automated financial trading, bulk data collection, and civilian behavioral analysis.



Despite its appearances, TIS-100 is a game!

  • Print and explore the TIS-100 reference manual, which details the inner-workings of the TIS-100 while evoking the aesthetics of a 1980’s computer manual!
  • Solve more than 45 puzzles, competing against your friends and the world to minimize your cycle, instruction, and node counts.
  • Design your own challenges in the TIS-100’s 3 sandboxes, including a “visual console” that lets you create your own games within the game!
  • Uncover the mysteries of the TIS-100… who created it, and for what purpose?
game_info.description: Repair the corrupted TIS-100 computer by programming data flow through interconnected nodes. Each puzzle presents input values that must be transformed and routed to output nodes following specific rules. Write simple assembly-like instructions (MOV, ADD, SUB, JMP) to process data streams. Master node communication and parallel processing to solve increasingly complex data transformation challenges. Complete all puzzles to unlock the secrets of the TIS-100!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between nodes and instructions
Space: Toggle instruction editing mode
Shift: Execute one step when paused
Z: Clear current instruction
Enter: Start game from menu
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic node programming and data flow. The test will program a simple pass-through operation where input values are moved directly to output without transformation. This validates that the core instruction system (MOV) works correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Write MOV instructions to transfer data from input nodes to output nodes. The strategy focuses on creating a simple pipeline: read from UP, write to DOWN. This tests the fundamental communication between nodes.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test is successful when input values reach the output nodes unmodified and the puzzle is marked as complete. The game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state when all required outputs are satisfied.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests complete puzzle solving with data transformation. This validates arithmetic operations (ADD, SUB) and conditional logic (JMP) work correctly. Tests the full instruction set and multi-node coordination.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Program nodes to read inputs, perform required arithmetic operations, and route results to correct outputs. Use multiple nodes working in parallel to transform data according to puzzle requirements. Implement loops and conditionals as needed.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully solve the active puzzle by meeting all output requirements. All test cases should pass with correct values at output nodes. Game reaches GAME_OVER_WIN when puzzle completion conditions are met.
